an4.19:1.1: “There are these four ways of going off course.
an4.19:1.3: One goes off course through desire . One goes off course through aversion. One goes off course through delusion. One goes off course through fear.
an4.19:1.4: These are the four ways of going off course.”
an4.19:2.1: “There are these four ways of not going off course.
an4.19:2.3: One doesn’t go off course through desire. One doesn’t go off course through aversion. One doesn’t go off course through delusion. One doesn’t go off course through fear.
an4.19:2.4: These are the four ways of not going off course.”
an4.19:3.3: transgress the Dhamma, your honor wanes,
an4.19:3.4: as in the dark fortnight, the moon.
an4.19:4.2: through desire, aversion, delusion, fear— transgress the Dhamma,
an4.19:4.4: as in the bright fortnight, the moon.
